Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W20X-00000-00MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - UP TO DATE DEVELOPMENTS WITH DPR CONSTRUCTION AND OTHERS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:50:20ZLexisNexisSTOCKMCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - UP TO DATE DEVELOPMENTS WITH DPR CONSTRUCTION AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC.

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. MORE THAN 160 YEARS OF BUILDING FORWARD

For more than 160 years, McCarthy has been shaped by people who came before, building a legacy grounded in craftsmanship, integrity and long-term vision. This second edition of the company's history book captures the evolution from its earliest roots to a modern, employee-owned organization committed to innovation, partnership and great experiences for everyone. Along the way, it traces the milestones, bold decisions and defining projects that helped establish McCarthy as one of the industry's most respected builders. It is both a reflection on where McCarthy has been and a celebration of the ownership mindset shaping where it goes next.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. BAYTOWN AREA WATER AUTHORITY AND BAYTOWN WATERWORKS PARTNERS BREAK GROUND ON EAST SURFACE WATER TREATMENT PLANT PHASE II

Baytown Area Water Authority and Baytown Waterworks Partners, a joint venture between McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. and Carollo Engineers, are pleased to announce they have broken ground on Phase II of the Baytown Area Water Authority East Surface Water Treatment Plant, a progressive design-build expansion that will increase the facility's treatment capacity from 6 million gallons per day (MGD) to 12 MGD. This milestone marks a major investment in long-term, reliable surface water supplies for the Baytown area.

Through a progressive design-build delivery, Baytown Waterworks Partners has collaborated closely with Baytown Area Water Authority, in conjunction with BGE Engineering who serves as the Owner's Advisor, from early design and will continue to collaborate through construction to advance technical solutions, manage risk, and efficiently deliver the expanded facilities. The plant expansion is expected to be fully functional at target capacity by summer 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. SERVICE AFTER SERVICE: VETERANS BUILDING HOUSTON'S FUTURE WITH MCCARTHY

When sailors and Marines arrived in Houston for its first-ever hosted Fleet Week, most people looked up - to the ships, the helicopters and the flyovers. At McCarthy, a lot of our partners look out - to the wharves, docks and water infrastructure they now help build along the same channels those vessels call home.

For veterans like Andy Garcia, Corey Everitte and John Walker, Fleet Week isn't just a celebration of military service. It's a reminder that their mission hasn't ended with military retirement; it has just evolved to helping build and protect the infrastructure that keeps a maritime city moving.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. TAKING PRIDE IN SAFETY: A CONVERSATION WITH BILLY NAYLOR

Having the privilege of leading national safety for McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. is one of the greatest honors of my life. It's something I'm extraordinarily proud of and something I never thought I'd accomplish. I want every employee to know there is not a day I take it for granted, not a day I don't take it seriously and not a day I'm not pulling hard for everyone to return home safely. Q: Serious Injury and Fatality (SIF) prevention is one of McCarthy's highest safety priorities for 2026. What makes SIF events different from other incidents, and what specific steps is McCarthy's national safety team taking this year to get in front of them before they happen?

A: SIF requires a mindset shift. That's the most important thing to understand about it.

For years, the construction industry was wired to think about safety through the lens of recordability. Our recordable rate is something we're mandated to submit on every proposal, every owner deliverable. And companies - including ours - have gotten very good at driving that number down. When I joined McCarthy in 2006, our rates were much higher. Reducing recordable incidents became the driver of our behaviors and the primary way we measured ourselves.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [4 Press Releases]

+ TURNER MARKS 124TH ANNIVERSARY WITH FOUNDERS' MONTH OF COMMUNITY IMPACT

In celebration of its founding in May 1902, Turner Construction Company is proud to launch its annual Founders' Month, a global effort that brings together more than 16,000 employees in service to the communities where they live and work.

The construction industry hired approximately four million workers in the United States but added only approximately 400,000 jobs in 2025. The Associated Builders and Contractors estimates the industry will need to attract roughly 365,000 net additional workers in 2026 to keep pace with demand, with skilled trades availability emerging as one of the most critical constraints on project delivery.

With that challenge in mind, this year's Founders' Month places an emphasis on strengthening the construction workforce pipeline - connecting community engagement with training, access, and career opportunities that help bring more people into the trades and support them in building long-term careers.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: BUILDING A SUSTAINABLE FUTURE TOGETHER

Throughout April, including Earth Day on April 22, Turner International Turkiye celebrated Earth Month across all 8 workplaces with a series of inspiring activities focused on sustainability and environmental awareness.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [1 Press Release]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC MOUNT SINAI MEDICAL CENTER REACHES TOPPING OUT MILESTONE FOR NEW WESTCHESTER EMERGENCY AND SPECIALTY CARE FACILITY

Construction crews have placed the final beam atop Mount Sinai Medical Center's new Westchester Freestanding Emergency Department, marking a major milestone in expanding access to high-quality, around-the-clock care for residents in western Miami-Dade County. The new facility will bring Mount Sinai's trusted emergency and specialty care closer to home for thousands of area families, helping reduce travel times and meet growing healthcare needs.

Located in Westchester, the four-story, 112,000 square-foot facility-designed by HuntonBrady Architects-will feature 26 exam bays, a Baker Act receiving unit, and advanced imaging services such as MRI, CT, and ultrasound. The project also features a detached two-story central energy plant and medical gas building, demolition of two existing structures and six acres of site improvements. Construction is expected to be complete late 2026.

"This milestone is a tribute to the skill and dedication of every crew member who's helped bring this facility to life," said Mike Peters, vice president and division manager at Brasfield & Gorrie. "Partnering with Mount Sinai Medical Center and HuntonBrady Architects on this important project highlights what can be achieved through teamwork and craftsmanship. Our crews take great pride in knowing their work will help make emergency and specialty care more accessible for families across Miami-Dade County."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Baytown Area Water Authority and Baytown Waterworks Partners, a joint venture between McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. and Carollo Engineers, are pleased to announce they have broken ground on Phase II of the Baytown Area Water Authority East Surface Water Treatment Plant, a progressive design-build expansion that will increase the facility's treatment capacity from 6 million gallons per day (MGD) to 12 MGD. This milestone marks a major investment in long-term, reliable surface water supplies for the Baytown area.

Through a progressive design-build delivery, Baytown Waterworks Partners has collaborated closely with Baytown Area Water Authority, in conjunction with BGE Engineering who serves as the Owner's Advisor, from early design and will continue to collaborate through construction to advance technical solutions, manage risk, and efficiently deliver the expanded facilities. The plant expansion is expected to be fully functional at target capacity by summer 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. SERVICE AFTER SERVICE: VETERANS BUILDING HOUSTON'S FUTURE WITH MCCARTHY

When sailors and Marines arrived in Houston for its first-ever hosted Fleet Week, most people looked up - to the ships, the helicopters and the flyovers. At McCarthy, a lot of our partners look out - to the wharves, docks and water infrastructure they now help build along the same channels those vessels call home.

For veterans like Andy Garcia, Corey Everitte and John Walker, Fleet Week isn't just a celebration of military service. It's a reminder that their mission hasn't ended with military retirement; it has just evolved to helping build and protect the infrastructure that keeps a maritime city moving.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. TAKING PRIDE IN SAFETY: A CONVERSATION WITH BILLY NAYLOR

Having the privilege of leading national safety for McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. is one of the greatest honors of my life. It's something I'm extraordinarily proud of and something I never thought I'd accomplish. I want every employee to know there is not a day I take it for granted, not a day I don't take it seriously and not a day I'm not pulling hard for everyone to return home safely. Q: Serious Injury and Fatality (SIF) prevention is one of McCarthy's highest safety priorities for 2026. What makes SIF events different from other incidents, and what specific steps is McCarthy's national safety team taking this year to get in front of them before they happen?

A: SIF requires a mindset shift. That's the most important thing to understand about it.

For years, the construction industry was wired to think about safety through the lens of recordability. Our recordable rate is something we're mandated to submit on every proposal, every owner deliverable. And companies - including ours - have gotten very good at driving that number down. When I joined McCarthy in 2006, our rates were much higher. Reducing recordable incidents became the driver of our behaviors and the primary way we measured ourselves.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [4 Press Releases]

+ TURNER MARKS 124TH ANNIVERSARY WITH FOUNDERS' MONTH OF COMMUNITY IMPACT

In celebration of its founding in May 1902, Turner Construction Company is proud to launch its annual Founders' Month, a global effort that brings together more than 16,000 employees in service to the communities where they live and work.

The construction industry hired approximately four million workers in the United States but added only approximately 400,000 jobs in 2025. The Associated Builders and Contractors estimates the industry will need to attract roughly 365,000 net additional workers in 2026 to keep pace with demand, with skilled trades availability emerging as one of the most critical constraints on project delivery.

With that challenge in mind, this year's Founders' Month places an emphasis on strengthening the construction workforce pipeline - connecting community engagement with training, access, and career opportunities that help bring more people into the trades and support them in building long-term careers.

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [1 Press Release]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC MOUNT SINAI MEDICAL CENTER REACHES TOPPING OUT MILESTONE FOR NEW WESTCHESTER EMERGENCY AND SPECIALTY CARE FACILITY

Construction crews have placed the final beam atop Mount Sinai Medical Center's new Westchester Freestanding Emergency Department, marking a major milestone in expanding access to high-quality, around-the-clock care for residents in western Miami-Dade County. The new facility will bring Mount Sinai's trusted emergency and specialty care closer to home for thousands of area families, helping reduce travel times and meet growing healthcare needs.

Located in Westchester, the four-story, 112,000 square-foot facility-designed by HuntonBrady Architects-will feature 26 exam bays, a Baker Act receiving unit, and advanced imaging services such as MRI, CT, and ultrasound. The project also features a detached two-story central energy plant and medical gas building, demolition of two existing structures and six acres of site improvements. Construction is expected to be complete late 2026.

"This milestone is a tribute to the skill and dedication of every crew member who's helped bring this facility to life," said Mike Peters, vice president and division manager at Brasfield & Gorrie. "Partnering with Mount Sinai Medical Center and HuntonBrady Architects on this important project highlights what can be achieved through teamwork and craftsmanship. Our crews take great pride in knowing their work will help make emergency and specialty care more accessible for families across Miami-Dade County."

urn:contentItem:6JK2-K5D3-S2R3-314X-00000-00MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:21ZLexisNexisSTOCKMCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY HOLDINGS

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF $3.7B CALIFORNIA TOWER

McCarthy Holdings has reached a significant construction milestone with the topping out of the $3.7 billion California Tower on February 6, 2026. This achievement comes a year and a half after breaking ground on the ambitious project in 2024, as reported by UC Davis Health. The California Tower is designed using a progressive design-build delivery method, marking a pivotal moment in the expansion of UC Davis' Sacramento campus.The California Tower is part of an extensive eight-year plan to double the size of the UC Davis campus, which is recognized as the largest health system capital expansion currently underway in the United States. Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. Notably, the tower will also introduce approximately 334 additional inpatient beds to enhance patient care capabilities.In a customary celebration, stakeholders in the project gathered to sign the beam, which was ceremonially placed to mark this important milestone. UC Davis Health anticipates the opening of the California Tower in 2030. This state-of-the-art building is the final component of UC Davis Health's Vision 2030 plan, which aims to increase the campus's total building space from 3.6 million square feet to over 7 million square feet.The California Tower is not McCarthy Holdings' only recent achievement in the healthcare sector. The company completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December. While healthcare construction has shown resilience, contributing to overall growth in construction planning at the end of 2025, early indicators from Dodge's first report of 2026 suggest a slowdown in healthcare project planning at the start of the year.As McCarthy Holdings continues to make strides in the healthcare construction sector, the successful topping out of the California Tower signifies an important step in fulfilling the growing demand for healthcare infrastructure. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching how these developments unfold as the company forges ahead with its ambitious projects.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD AVIATION OPERATIONS IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations in the Carolinas, effective immediately. Johnson's new role will see him leading and expanding the company’s aviation sector across North and South Carolina, building on a career that spans over 19 years in the construction industry.As of 1st April 2026, Trent Johnson’s promotion is a reflection of his extensive experience, including more than 12 and a half years with Balfour Beatty. During his tenure, he has successfully contributed to the delivery of 22 aviation projects, ranging from targeted renovations to complex, multi-phase programmes. His leadership has been pivotal in enhancing Balfour Beatty’s aviation presence, notably at Raleigh-Durham International Airport (RDU) and Albert J. Ellis Airport.In his new capacity, Johnson will continue to support Balfour Beatty’s existing aviation clients while expanding the company’s footprint at additional regional and international airports throughout the Carolinas. His focus will remain on assisting project teams in navigating the complexities of active airport environments, while prioritizing safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination.Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, commented on Johnson’s promotion, stating, “Trent has built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner within our local aviation operations. His ability to guide teams through the complexities of active airport environments has been a key driver of our success in the region.” Sandeen expressed confidence that Johnson will play a crucial role in shaping the next phase of growth for Balfour Beatty’s aviation portfolio.Trent Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and is also known for his community involvement, particularly with the Make-A-Wish Foundation. His steady leadership style and collaborative approach have helped Balfour Beatty deliver projects that align with long-term regional growth and infrastructure priorities.

+ MAINMARK UK JOINS BALFOUR BEATTY'S CP7 FRAMEWORK FOR RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE

Ground engineering specialist Mainmark UK has secured a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the company to support the delivery of key rail infrastructure works across Network Rail’s latest five-year investment programme. This investment period, known as Control Period 7 (CP7), spans from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an allocation of approximately £43–45 billion across Great Britain.The CP7 programme focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway. Investment priorities during CP7 include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives aimed at enhancing both freight and passenger performance across the network.As part of the CP7 Framework, contractors will engage in a variety of works aligned with Network Rail’s Strategic Business Plan. These activities will encompass low-complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small-scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will be involved in geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among others.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration with Balfour Beatty, stating, “We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans.”Mainmark is known for its innovative ground engineering solutions, particularly its Teretek® resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground conditions and fills subsurface voids without extensive excavation. This technology is crucial for maintaining the integrity of rail infrastructure, especially as heavier freight loads and severe weather events put increasing pressure on railway assets. With its appointment to the CP7 Framework, Mainmark UK is set to play a vital role in ensuring the resilience and reliability of the UK's rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY'S GROWTH FUELED BY RECORD ORDER BOOK

Balfour Beatty PLC has reported impressive growth, driven by its robust performance in the construction sector, particularly in power transmission projects. The company’s market valuation has soared nearly two-thirds over the past year, a noteworthy achievement for a FTSE 250 constituent. This growth trajectory is largely attributed to a record order book and a 9 percent increase in underlying earnings per share, which reached 47.6 pence.On 11th March 2026, the construction group revealed that its top-line growth was significantly supported by the increasing demand for power transmission in the UK and a surge in building projects in the US. Operating profits within Balfour Beatty's support services segment saw a remarkable increase of one-third, amounting to £122 million, alongside an 80 basis point rise in the underlying margin to 8.5 percent. The company’s UK construction division achieved its margin targets ahead of schedule, underscoring its operational efficiency.In the US market, Balfour Beatty has capitalized on rising building volumes although it faced challenges due to cost overruns in a project within its Civils arm. The dual market strategy appears to be paying off, as the company navigates through these hurdles while maintaining a focus on profitability. According to company representatives, the first floor will serve as an event and training center designed to host gatherings and professional development opportunities, featuring a large banquet room, an auditorium, and several meeting and training rooms.The remaining three floors are planned for office space that will support Brasfield & Gorrie’s expanding national operations. Company leaders say the additional space will allow them to accommodate new employees while leaving room for future growth. In addition to the new building, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to add a fourth level to its existing parking deck, creating approximately 125 additional parking spaces for employees and visitors.As part of the expansion, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to create 80 new full-time jobs headquartered in Birmingham, with an expected average salary of about $82,000. During the council committee discussion, company leaders emphasized that the new roles will help support the firm’s operations across the country while keeping its headquarters firmly rooted in Birmingham. They stated, 'It will be jobs headquartered here in Birmingham to support our field and our operations. We’re really growing, and we’re committed to staying in Birmingham.'The project represents an estimated $64.5 million investment. Under the proposed agreement, the City of Birmingham would provide financial incentives of up to $80,000 to support the project. The proposal received approval from the council committee and will now move to the full Birmingham City Council for final consideration. If approved, company leaders say construction on the expansion could be completed by late 2027.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE RECEIVES $12M TAX INCENTIVE FOR JEFFERSON COUNTY PROJECT

Birmingham-based construction firm Brasfield & Gorrie has secured a substantial economic incentive package from state officials, aimed at bolstering its $65 million project in Jefferson County. On 20th February 2026, the Alabama Department of Commerce approved nearly $12 million in tax credits for the company, which includes $9.69 million in investment tax credits and $2.24 million in jobs tax credits.The incentives are part of a strategic initiative to promote economic development in the region and are expected to play a significant role in the execution of Brasfield & Gorrie's ambitious project. This funding will not only support the immediate development but also contribute to job creation and local investment, which aligns with the state's broader economic goals.Brasfield & Gorrie, a privately held firm recognized for its extensive portfolio in construction and project management, has been a key player in Alabama's construction landscape. The approval of these tax credits underscores the state's commitment to fostering growth within its local businesses, particularly in the construction sector, which has faced challenges amid fluctuating economic conditions.The $64.6 million project will likely enhance Brasfield & Gorrie's operational capabilities and expand its service offerings, further solidifying its market position. As the company moves forward with the development, stakeholders will be keenly observing how these incentives translate into tangible benefits for both Brasfield & Gorrie and the Jefferson County community.In conclusion, the Alabama Department of Commerce's support through this tax incentive package marks a significant milestone for Brasfield & Gorrie, reinforcing its role in driving economic progress in the region. The anticipated outcomes of this project will not only reflect on the company's growth trajectory but also on the overall economic vitality of Birmingham and its surrounding areas.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK8-3TC3-SBJX-V3X9-00000-00INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:14ZLexisNexisINVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

NEW YORK, May 14, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Pomerantz LLP is investigating claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation ("Primoris" or the "Company") (NYSE: PRIM ). Such investors are advised to contact Danielle Peyton at [email protected] or 646-581-9980, ext. 7980.


The investigation concerns whether Primoris and certain of its officers and/or directors have engaged in securities fraud or other unlawful business practices.
[Click here for information about joining the class action]
On May 5, 2026, Primoris issued a press release reporting its financial results for the first quarter of 2026. Primoris reported results below analyst expectations and slashed full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million. Primoris attributed the reduction to lower renewable energy activity, delayed project starts, and increased costs on renewable energy projects.
On this news, Primoris's stock price fell $101.69 per share, or 50.11%, to close at $101.23 per share on May 6, 2026.
Pomerantz LLP, with offices in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, London, Paris, and Tel Aviv, is acknowledged as one of the premier firms in the areas of corporate, securities, and antitrust class litigation. Founded by the late Abraham L. Pomerantz, known as the dean of the class action bar, Pomerantz pioneered the field of securities class actions. Today, more than 85 years later, Pomerantz continues in the tradition he established, fighting for the rights of the victims of securities fraud, breaches of fiduciary duty, and corporate misconduct. The Firm has recovered numerous multimillion-dollar damages awards on behalf of class members. See .
Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.
CONTACT:
Danielle Peyton
Pomerantz LLP
[email protected]
646-581-9980 ext. 7980
SOURCE Pomerantz LLP

urn:contentItem:6JJX-DGD3-RSP4-R4K3-00000-00BAKER MCKENZIE - UPDATED SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:50:20ZLexisNexisSTOCKBAKER MCKENZIE - UPDATED SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: BAKER MCKENZIE

PermID4297065357Websitehttps://www.bakermckenzie.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress300 East Randolph Street, Suite 5000, Chicago, Illinois 60601, United States

ACTIVITIES:

BakerMcKenzie, founded in 1949, is a global law firm headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. With over 6,000 lawyers across 77 offices in 46 countries, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in tax, M&A, and dispute resolution. Generating over $3 billion annually, its a leader in cross-border transactions, advising on global ESG compliance. In 2024, Baker McKenzie enhanced its AI and digital transformation practices, per its website. Its culture emphasizes inclusion, collaboration, and pro bono work, with a top ranking in employee benefits by Legal 500. Competing with DLA Piper, Baker McKenzie stands out for its global reach and local expertise. Recent initiatives include sustainable finance advisory and cloud-based legal tech, aligning with market trends. The Baker & McKenzie Foundation supports community programs, reinforcing its social commitment.

+ BAKER MCKENZIE [3 News That Matters]

+ BAKER MCKENZIE EXPANDS DISPUTE RESOLUTION TEAM IN MEXICO WITH NEW PARTNER

Baker McKenzie has announced the addition of a new partner to its dispute resolution team in Mexico, strengthening the firm's regional capabilities. Christian brings more than twenty years of experience advising on arbitration, civil, commercial and family matters and will join as part of a push to deepen cross-border dispute coverage amid Mexico's reform-driven landscape.On 4th May 2026, the firm highlighted Christian's work across jurisdictions and high-profile cases, including advising the Mexican Congress on freedom of expression and damages, and handling technology, artificial intelligence and blockchain-related disputes. His dual role as mediator and litigator will be central to delivering robust representation in complex matters.The appointment enhances Baker McKenzie's cross-border capacity in the Americas, Europe and beyond. Before joining the firm, Christian ran Zinser Legal since 2006 and holds an LLB from Universidad Anahuac del Norte, an LLM from Universidad Panamericana, and an executive MBA from Quantic School of Business and Technology.

+ BAKER MCKENZIE ADVISES VIRGIN MEDIA O2 ON 10-YEAR PPA WITH EGG POWER

Baker McKenzie has advised Virgin Media O2 on its latest long-term Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with egg Power. The agreement, formalized on the 5th of May 2026, marks a significant step in Virgin Media O2's commitment to sustainable energy solutions.Under the 10-year term, egg Power will deliver solar energy from its forthcoming solar farm in Suffolk, due to begin operations in 2027. The renewable energy is projected to account for about 5% of Virgin Media O2's total energy requirements.This PPA builds on Virgin Media O2's broader clean energy strategy, which also includes a prior wind energy arrangement with The Renewables Infrastructure Group in 2025, facilitated by Baker McKenzie. Together, these two deals are expected to meet around 20% of the company’s energy demand, underscoring its decarbonisation push. The Baker McKenzie team was led by Energy & Infrastructure Partner James Wyatt, with Associate Alex Tam, supported by Consultant Andrew Hedges and Trainee Kayan Sayeed. Wyatt said, 'This agreement represents a further step in Virgin Media O2's transition toward sustainable energy procurement, and we were pleased to support our client on another strategically important PPA, reflecting our continued focus on advising on complex energy and decarbonisation driven projects.'

+ DAILY MAIL OWNER TO SELL DATA ARM FOR ABOUT $1 BILLION, BAKER MCKENZIE ADVISES

The Daily Mail's owner has announced plans to sell its data business for approximately $1 billion, with Baker McKenzie providing critical guidance throughout the process. The transaction is set for completion by the end of this fiscal year and is expected to close on or around the 1st of May, 2026, as the group lines up with a broader strategy to streamline operations and pivot toward opportunities that leverage its media assets. Analysts say divesting the data arm could free resources to bolster the core media units that have historically driven revenue, while Baker McKenzie’s role underscores the transaction's likely regulatory and market complexities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WHITE & CASE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ WHITE & CASE APPOINTS ANDREA MEREDIZ BASHAM TO LEAD EXPANSION OF M&A PRACTICE

White &Case announced the appointment of Andrea Merediz Basham as a partner in its Global Mergers & Acquisitions Practice, based in New York, as the firm continues to expand its U.S. private capital and M&A capabilities. This strategic move comes on the 13th of April, 2026, amid rising client demand for sophisticated advisory services in the M&A sector.Basham brings extensive experience advising international corporates and private capital investors, including sovereign wealth funds, on complex transactions such as mergers and acquisitions, joint ventures, debt and equity investments, and co-investment structures. She joins White & Case from Freshfields, where she served as a partner.The hire aligns with the firm’s broader strategy to strengthen its M&A platform, which has grown significantly over the past year. White & Case has welcomed more than 25 lateral partners globally, with notable additions in New York, Houston and Boston. Recent U.S. M&A hires include Jennifer Cheng and Paul Tiger in New York, as well as Gabriel Salinas and Ian Goldberg in Houston.

+ WHITE & CASE BOLSTERS LIFE SCIENCES IP LITIGATION WITH FOUR PARTNER HIRES

White & Case LLP has expanded its Global Intellectual Property Practice and its Global Life Sciences and Healthcare Industry Group with four seasoned life sciences trial litigators — Elizabeth Holland, Nicholas Mitrokostas, William James, and Daniel Margolis — who will operate from New York, Washington, DC, and Boston. The move was announced on Jan. 15, 2026. Chair Heather McDevitt described the hires as a strategic enhancement that will help clients navigate rapid life sciences innovation and a stringent regulatory landscape. Holland joins as Head of the Global Life Sciences IP Litigation Practice, bringing extensive experience in patent infringement litigations and licensing disputes as lead trial counsel for major clients. Mitrokostas, named Co-Head of the Global Life Sciences and Healthcare Industry Group, is recognized for handling high-stakes patent and trade secret disputes in federal courts and arbitrations, with a focus on late-stage drug development. James and Margolis contribute deep trial experience in Hatch-Waxman and biotechnology matters, strengthening White & Case’s federal court and regulatory capabilities. Yar Chaikovsky, head of the Global IP Practice, noted the team’s renowned trial advocacy would give the firm a competitive edge in high-stakes matters. The recruitment of these four partners marks the seventh high-profile partner addition White & Case has announced in the past year as the firm pursues an aggressive expansion across key practices to broaden its global footprint.

+ WHITE & CASE REAPPOINTS WILL STAWELL AS SYDNEY PARTNER TO BOLSTER DEBT FINANCE PRACTICE

White & Case LLP has announced the return of Will Stawell as a partner in its Sydney office, part of a broader effort to bolster its Global Debt Finance Practice and Global Financial Institutions Industry Group in Australia. The move is effective immediately and highlights the firm’s push to deepen its capabilities in the local financial market.Eliza McDougall, head of the Global Debt Finance Practice, noted that the firm’s fully integrated global finance offering spanning the Americas, EMEA and Asia-Pacific enables it to provide tailored counsel on today’s credit products and markets. This appointment strengthens those cross-border capabilities in Australia.Stawell previously worked as an associate at White & Case in New York from 2013 to 2016 and returns with nearly two decades of experience in debt finance, including acquisition finance, leverage finance and restructurings, which are central to clients navigating today’s capital markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JONES DAY [3 News That Matters]

+ JONES DAY PARTNER ANNA DWYER NAMED GLOBEST. RISING STAR IN 2026 WOMEN OF INFLUENCE

Anna Dwyer, a partner in Jones Day's Real Estate Practice, has been named a GlobeSt. Rising Star as part of the 2026 Women of Influence Awards, recognizing leadership in the commercial real estate sector. Dwyer handles joint ventures, financing, leasing, acquisitions, and dispositions across mixed-use, retail, office, and industrial properties for a diverse client roster.On the 21st of April 2026, it was announced that Dwyer represents real estate investment managers, digital infrastructure providers, and national lenders, providing end-to-end counsel from deal structuring to negotiation and risk allocation.Among her notable matters, she represented a leading real estate investment manager in a strategic joint venture with a publicly traded REIT to acquire and operate a portfolio of stabilized multifamily communities across three states. She also co-led a Jones Day team advising CBRE Investment Management Indirect in acquiring a meaningful interest in a retail asset portfolio valued at over $1 billion. In another matter, she guided a data center, cloud, and interconnection services provider through the acquisition and financing of its headquarters, underscoring the intersection of real estate and digital infrastructure. Beyond her deal work, Dwyer commits to education and civic engagement as an alumni mentor for the Pritzker Northwestern School of Law Pre-Law Academy, and as an active member of the Reunion Committee and WITS initiatives.

+ JONES DAY ANNOUNCES ADDITION OF PAUL STANCIL TO LITIGATION PRACTICE

Jones Day announced the addition of Paul Stancil as Of Counsel in its Business & Tort Litigation Practice, based in the Dallas office. The appointment is effective as of 10th April 2026 and enhances the firm's capability in complex civil disputes, including antitrust, trade secret, and commercial matters. Stancil brings more than 25 years of experience across U.S. federal and Texas state courts, having served as trial counsel in numerous significant cases. He notably co-led an antitrust matter in the hospitality sector and has spent more than 15 years as a tenured law professor, melding practical courtroom skill with academic insight. "As John Majoras, co-leader of the firm's Business & Tort Litigation Practice, said, 'Paul has significant trial experience in complex commercial disputes and high-stakes antitrust litigation.'" He will join a global team of more than 400 trial lawyers addressing business and government disputes, torts, and civil matters from pre-suit counseling through trial and appeal. Stancil also expressed enthusiasm: "Jones Day has an extraordinary track record of success working on the most complex and high-stakes commercial disputes across Texas and around the world each year." He will collaborate with colleagues across Dallas and the firm's international offices on complex commercial litigation and antitrust cases.

+ JONES DAY EXPANDS APPEALS PRACTICE WITH FOUR FORMER SUPREME COURT CLERKS

JonesDay is expanding its issues and appeals practice by hiring four former U.S. Supreme Court clerks as associates, announced in a Mar. 30 press release. The new hires, who will join the firm, are Elise Kostial, Kari Lorentson, Seanhenry VanDyke, and Alexis Zhang, each of whom clerked during the October 2024 term. The release is dated Apr. 1, 2026.The firm has built a reputation for attracting top legal talent, having recruited 100 Supreme Court clerks since the October 2011 term. The current cohort is smaller than recent cycles, which included 10 clerks from the October 2023 term, eight from the October 2022 term, and seven from the October 2021 term.All four clerks served for Republican-appointed justices: Kostial for Chief Justice Roberts, Lorentson for Justice Barrett, VanDyke for Justice Kavanaugh, and Zhang for Justice Thomas. The hires reflect the firm's strategic focus on issues tied to today’s political and judicial climate.As the four join, Jones Day expects to leverage their Supreme Court experience to tackle complex matters and strengthen its competitive position in the market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DENTONS US LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DENTONS ADVISES AD PORTS GROUP ON AED 650 MILLION KEZAD WAREHOUSES SALE TO ALDAR

Dentons has advised AD Ports Group, a leading global enabler of trade, industry and logistics solutions, on the sale of three warehouses in KEZAD Logistics Park in Abu Dhabi to Aldar for AED 650 million (US$177 million). The transaction, completed on 27th April 2026, comprises three warehouses with a total leasable area of approximately 161,000 square metres. This sale aligns with AD Ports Group’s strategy to selectively monetize real estate assets and reinvest in its core business to enhance shareholder value. It marks the second collaboration between AD Ports Group and Aldar in logistics warehouse assets, following an earlier acquisition in November 2025, also facilitated by Dentons. Hazel Shakur Quinn, who led the transaction at Dentons, stated, "This is another great example of AD Ports Group executing its asset optimisation strategy with real discipline by recycling capital into higher-growth opportunities while continuing to attract strong investor interest." The deal underscores robust demand for high-quality logistics and industrial assets in Abu Dhabi, and the Dentons team included Hazel Shakur Quinn (Partner and Head of Real Estate – Middle East) and Joe Carroll (Counsel, Real Estate), among others. Dentons congratulates AD Ports Group and Aldar on the completion of this landmark transaction.

+ DENTONS PROMOTES FOUR PARTNERS ACROSS UKIME REGION TO SUPPORT GROWTH

London — Dentons, the world's largest global law firm, has announced the promotion of four partners across the UKIME Region, with the promotions taking effect on 1 May 2026. The moves follow Dentons' ongoing expansion, including 14 new partners in 2025 and a further three joining the firm in 2026 to date.Andrew Clarke, who specializes in Projects in Saudi Arabia, joined Dentons in 2024 and is leading cross-border infrastructure investment and the firm's growth in Saudi Arabia.Emma Frost, based in London, has more than 20 years with the firm and is known for complex real estate development projects.Hannah Harris specializes in Employment law; she joined in 2024 and handles multi-jurisdictional employment matters.Rukia Khatun, in Technology, Media & Telecoms, has spent 12 years at Dentons, rising from trainee to partner, and is known for work with retail and public sector clients.Paul Jarvis, Dentons' CEO for UKIME, said, "These promotions reflect some of our strategic growth priorities across the region. Our new partners comprise a mix of long-serving Dentons colleagues and relatively recent hires who have added immense value to the firm in a short period of time. Between them, they illustrate various routes to partnership. Congratulations to all our new partners."These promotions highlight Dentons' commitment to recognizing talent within the firm and to strengthening service capabilities across key sectors in the UKIME region.

+ DENTONS ADVISES GLOBAL RELAY ON LANDMARK ACQUISITION OF 45 CANNON STREET

Dentons, the world's largest global law firm, advised Global Relay on the acquisition and financing of 45 Cannon Street in the City of London. The transaction was finalized on the 23rd of April, 2026 and marks a key step for Global Relay in expanding its compliance-solutions business into new sectors.The 90,000-square-foot building will serve Global Relay as its London headquarters, with Global Relay occupying a substantial portion and the remainder fully leased to tenants including DWS Group."This acquisition reflects where Global Relay is headed. We have built a world-class business serving some of the most highly regulated industries, and we are increasingly extending our reach beyond those traditional markets. 45 Cannon Street is a statement of that ambition."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT [3 News That Matters]

+ NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT TOWER REDEFINES OFFICE DESIGN STANDARDS IN HOUSTON

On 5th May 2026 Norton Rose Fulbright unveiled its Houston Tower, designed to redefine the tenant experience by blending productivity with wellness through a suite of modern amenities.The tower features wellness programs, collaborative spaces, and advanced technology, with a design that prioritizes open spaces, natural light, and ergonomic furnishings to support the workday.The project reflects a broader shift toward hybrid models, offering green spaces, recreational areas, and conference facilities equipped for seamless communication, aimed at attracting top tenants and strengthening retention.

+ NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT ADDS ACCLAIMED APPELLATE VETERAN ANNE JOHNSON TO DALLAS TEAM

Norton Rose Fulbright has welcomed veteran appellate lawyer Anne Johnson to its Dallas office, marking a significant addition to its legal team. Johnson, who joined the firm earlier this month, brings a wealth of experience and a strong reputation as one of Texas's foremost appellate attorneys, aligning with rising demand for sophisticated appellate counsel in high-stakes cases.With four years at Tillotson Johnson & Patton, Johnson has established herself as a leader in appellate advocacy, focusing on complex litigation and post-trial proceedings. Since launching her career at Haynes Boone—where she practiced for 26 years and held numerous leadership roles—Johnson has developed a keen understanding of appellate strategies that resonate with clients. Jeff Cody, Norton Rose's global and U.S. managing partner, said, "Anne brings a depth of appellate experience that has earned her a strong reputation among clients facing the most consequential stages of disputes." She will lead the U.S. appellate practice at the firm, collaborating closely with trial teams to shape cohesive litigation strategies from the early stages through to appeals.

+ NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT AND LATHAM & WATKINS GUIDE $2.2 BILLION MASDAR–TOTALENERGIES ONSHORE JV

Norton Rose Fulbright and Latham & Watkins have advised on a $2.2 billion joint venture between Masdar and TotalEnergies. The deal aims to consolidate their onshore renewables footprint across nine Asian markets and was announced on the 21st of April 2026. It remains subject to regulatory approvals.The joint venture will serve as the exclusive platform to develop, build, own, and operate onshore solar, wind, and battery storage projects in Azerbaijan, Indonesia, Japan, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Uzbekistan. It targets 3 GW of operational capacity with a further 6 GW under development by 2030, signaling a meaningful step in the energy transition.NRF advised Masdar with a London-led team anchored by Charles Whitney and Chris Grieves, while Latham & Watkins counseled TotalEnergies, led by Beatrice Lo and Ed Barnett.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LINKLATERS LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LINKLATERS NAMES TOBY GRIMSTONE GLOBAL BANKING CO-HEAD, SUCCEEDING REBECCA JARVIS

Linklaters has announced the appointment of Toby Grimstone, partner in the firm’s London office, as its new global co-head of banking, alongside the existing global co-head Ben Crosse. Grimstone succeeds Rebecca Jarvis, who will retire at the end of April after 29 years with the firm.Grimstone, who currently co-heads corporate and structured lending, specializes in international banking deals, advising banks and borrowers in syndicated and secured financings. His expertise spans corporate lending, event-driven transactions, structured finance, and limited recourse financings, and he has built strong relationships with major banking clients, enhancing Linklaters’ reputation.Ben Crosse, Global Co-Head of Linklaters’ Banking Practice, expressed confidence in Grimstone, saying, "Toby is a highly experienced and trusted lawyer with a strong reputation for delivering solutions for clients, especially in complex international situations." He added the firm remains committed to maintaining its status as a leading player in the banking sector under Grimstone's guidance and acknowledged Jarvis's significant contributions, noting her role in many of the firm’s largest and most complex restructurings.Linklaters’ global banking practice encompasses Leveraged Finance, Corporate and Structured Lending, and Restructuring and Insolvency. These teams collaborate effectively to advise banks, credit funds, and other financial institutions across diverse sectors on domestic and international financing transactions. The practice is consistently ranked tier 1 in major legal directories worldwide and has experienced notable growth over the past year.Recent developments within the banking practice at Linklaters include the lateral hires of several professionals, such as Angel Quek and Peter Hayes in London, and Francois Kopf and Mathieu Della Vittoria in Paris. In addition, the firm has promoted five new partners and elevated seven lawyers to counsel within its global banking practice in 2026, signaling a robust expansion strategy in a competitive market.

+ LINKLATERS ADVISES GLENCORE IN TULLOW OIL DEBT REFINANCING

Linklaters advised Glencore as part of the refinancing of Tullow Oil's debt, including $1.285 billion of senior secured notes and $400 million of Glencore junior notes. The transaction was implemented consensually with Glencore through a consent solicitation of the senior secured notes, which drew 99.7% support from noteholders.The refinancing is designed to extend Tullow's debt maturity profile, support its investment programme, and provide a stable platform for realising asset value. Key elements include extending the maturity of the senior secured notes to 15th November 2028 and delivering a $100 million debt paydown. Glencore's $400 million secured notes facility was replaced with $423 million in Glencore junior notes maturing on 15th May 2030, along with a $100 million super senior revolving cargo prepayment facility.Additionally, Tullow Oil has strengthened its governance by appointing at least three new independent non-executive directors and forming a board sub-committee to oversee a disciplined value-maximisation process across the asset base. The transaction also highlights Linklaters' leadership in complex cross-border restructurings, with the team led by Rowland Light, Toby Grimstone, and Ewan Smith.

+ SUN PHARMA TO ACQUIRE ORGANON IN $11.75 BILLION ALL-CASH DEAL, EXPANDING GLOBAL FOOTPRINT

Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited has unveiled an all cash acquisition of Organon & Co in a deal valued at 11.75 billion dollars, announced on 27th of April 2026. The transaction is expected to position Sun Pharma among the top 25 global pharmaceutical companies with projected annual revenues of about 12.4 billion dollars after closing. The deal will be completed through Sun Pharmaceutical Holdings, a U.S.-based subsidiary. Legal advisory for Sun Pharma includes White & Case LLP as the primary advisor, while AZB & Partners handles Indian law aspects, with Senior Partner Vaidhyanadhan Iyer and Partner Jeet Chaudhuri among others guiding financing law. Organon, headquartered in the United States, focuses on women’s health, biosimilars and other therapies. The deal marks a strategic expansion for Sun Pharma, which already leads in the specialty generic sector and operates in over 100 countries with revenue around 6.2 billion. The involvement of top firms and financing banks underscores the significance of the transaction in reshaping the pharmaceutical landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FRESHFIELDS BRUCKHAUS DERINGER [3 News That Matters]

+ FRESHFIELDS GUIDES ADVENT AND CINVEN THROUGH KONE-TK ELEVATOR MERGER REGULATORY PATH

Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer is advising Advent International and Cinven on the regulatory aspects of the proposed Kone Corporation and TK Elevator merger. On 30th April 2026, Advent and Cinven signed an agreement with Kone to combine the businesses in a cash and share transaction that is expected to create a leading global elevator and escalator group.The deal values TK Elevator at about EUR 29.4 billion in enterprise value. The enlarged group will employ more than 100,000 people across more than 100 countries and is forecast to generate around EUR 20.5 billion in annual sales, with its headquarters in Finland.Freshfields leads the regulatory approvals process, with Partners Martin Klusmann in Dusseldorf and Alastair Chapman in London at the helm, supported by Jenn Mellott and Christine Wilson in Washington and Hazel Yin with Freshfields RuiMin in Shanghai. The firm also advised on an IPO option considered for TKE, with a capital markets team headed by Christoph Gleske and Daniel von Bulow in Frankfurt and contributions from Simone Bono in London and Dirk-Jan Smit in Amsterdam.

+ FRESHFIELDS BRUCKHAUS DERINGER AND ANTHROPIC PARTNER TO DEVELOP AI TOOLS FOR LEGAL SERVICES

WASHINGTON, April 23rd 2026 — Global law firm Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer and artificial intelligence startup Anthropic announced a partnership to develop AI tools tailored for the legal sector, part of a broader push by top firms to leverage generative AI for efficiency and client service.The agreement calls for Freshfields to collaborate with Anthropic's legal team to create new AI applications and to receive early access to future models and products, with financial terms not disclosed. Freshfields has already deployed Anthropic's Claude internally and plans to extend usage to Cowork, Anthropic's autonomous AI platform which recently added a legal plug-in.The collaboration underscores a growing market for AI in law, where tools aim to speed legal research, contract drafting, and document preparation. The industry has drawn attention from rivals and investors, with other startups achieving high valuations and legal-focused products from players such as Thomson Reuters. Freshfields is positioned as an early innovator and valued partner in this space. Still, the legal use of AI raises accuracy concerns. High-profile missteps have prompted cautions about hallucinated sources. Freshfields says it will address these risks as it expands its AI work to improve research, contract reviews, drafting, and internal workflow automation.

+ DUTCH COURT AFFIRMS NOVARTIS ENTRESTO PATENT, DEFEATS SYNTHON CHALLENGE

Novartis has secured a decisive legal victory defending its Entresto patent in the Dutch Court of Appeal, as the court dismissed Synthon's nullity challenge and upheld EP 1 467 728 protecting the valsartan–sacubitril combination and its supplementary protection certificate (SPC). The decision, issued on January 12, 2026, represents the first Europe-wide ruling on the patent in the current proceedings, with parallel actions still advancing in the United Kingdom. Entresto is used to treat chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction in adults and aims to reduce cardiovascular death and hospitalization risk.Synthon had argued that the patent lacked an inventive step and should be revoked, which would have undermined the SPC. The Court nonetheless found that the specific combination arose from non-obvious properties of angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs) and NEP inhibitors when used together, and that the patent credibly demonstrated improved therapeutic effects over valsartan alone, without requiring proof of synergy. The panel, led by presiding judge J. I. de Vreese-Rood, also noted that prior disclosures in the literature did not render the claimed subject matter obvious at the priority date.Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer represented Novartis in both the trial and appeal, with Amsterdam-based partners and specialists contributing to the defense, including Rutger Kleemans and patent attorney Jetze Beeksma. The ruling reinforces Novartis's position in the European market for chronic heart failure therapies and may influence future IP disputes in the sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HERBERT SMITH FREEHILLS LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LAZARD TO ACQUIRE CAMPBELL LUTYENS FOR $575 MILLION, EXPANDING ADVISORY PLATFORM

Lazard Ltd. has agreed to acquire Campbell Lutyens & Co. Ltd. for $575 million, expanding Lazard's financial advisory capabilities with a focus on private equity fundraising and secondary advisory. The deal, announced on 30th April 2026, aims to broaden Lazard's reach in private markets and infrastructure advisory by leveraging Campbell Lutyens' established relationships and industry knowledge. Herbert Smith Freehills advised Campbell Lutyens on the transaction, and Lazard expects the combination to deliver synergies and expanded client services across its global platform.

+ HERBERT SMITH FREEHILLS PROMOTES EIGHT LAWYERS TO EXECUTIVE COUNSEL IN AUSTRALIA

Herbert Smith Freehills has promoted eight Australian lawyers to executive counsel as part of its latest global round, with the promotions taking effect from 1st May 2026. The moves reinforce leadership across the firm's Australian offices in Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane.Across seven practice areas, including projects, employment, competition, tax, corporate governance and real estate, the eight new executive counsels will bring deep expertise to clients and the firm alike, with additional capability in energy, industrial relations and safety.Executive partner for Australia and Asia Kristin Stammer said the promotions reflect the high calibre of the lawyers and their contributions to clients and the firm, underscoring the firm's commitment to nurturing talent. 'Our new executive counsel are highly skilled and have been recognised for their outstanding contribution to our clients and firm,' Stammer said.

+ HERBERT SMITH FREEHILLS PROMOTES BELFAST LEAD TO ALSP MANAGING PARTNER

On the 28th of April 2026, Herbert Smith Freehills announced the promotion of its Belfast lead to managing partner of the firm's ALSP division. The move is designed to bolster ALSP delivery and client service through strengthened regional leadership.The appointee brings deep ALSP experience, and the Belfast office is expected to drive technology-enabled services and cross-practice collaboration as HSF expands its ALSP offerings. The firm is also increasing investment in technology and talent to deliver tailored, cost-efficient solutions for complex legal challenges.
